decisions.title: Spirit of Kiro
decisions.summary: Infinite crafting workshop game demonstrating AI-powered game development.

decisions.title: Frontend Systems Architecture
decisions.summary: The client uses a system-based architecture where independent systems communicate via events.

decisions.title: Message-Driven Communication
decisions.summary: Consistent naming patterns and organized server handlers for WebSocket messages.

decisions.title: Infrastructure as Code
decisions.summary: Each service has its own `iac/` directory containing CloudFormation YAML templates, deployment scripts, and environment-specific configurations.

decisions.title: Testing Structure
decisions.summary: Server tests are placed in `__tests__/` directories alongside source code, while client tests use Vitest with Vue Test Utils.

decisions.title: File Naming Conventions
decisions.summary: Conventions for file names, component names, and message types: kebab-case; for JavaScript/TypeScript variables and functions: camelCase; for Vue components and TypeScript interfaces: PascalCase.

decisions.title: Bun, Docker/Podman, and TypeScript
decisions.summary: Bun is chosen as the primary JavaScript runtime for both client and server development. Docker/Podman is used for containerized development with compose orchestration. TypeScript is implemented for type safety throughout the codebase.

decisions.title: Vue.js 3, Pinia, and Vite
decisions.summary: Vue.js 3 is selected as the component-based UI framework, utilizing the Composition API. Pinia is chosen for state management of reactive game state. Vite is used as the build tool and development server.

decisions.title: Bun WebSocket Server, AWS SDK, and Redis
decisions.summary: Bun WebSocket Server is used for real-time bidirectional communication. AWS SDK is integrated for AWS service interaction. Redis is employed for caching and session management.

decisions.title: Amazon Bedrock, DynamoDB, Cognito, S3, CloudFront, and MemoryDB
decisions.summary: Amazon Bedrock is integrated for AI model support. DynamoDB is chosen for game state and inventory persistence. Amazon Cognito is used for user authentication and authorization. S3 and CloudFront are selected for asset storage and content delivery. MemoryDB is utilized as a vector database for image matching.

decisions.title: Root, Client, Server, and Infrastructure Setup Commands
decisions.summary: Various development commands are outlined for different levels and purposes, including root-level commands for starting both client and server in development mode, client commands for development server start, production build, preview, unit tests, linting, and code formatting, server commands for starting the server with hot reload, running tests with JUnit output, and setting up local DynamoDB tables, and infrastructure setup commands for deploying Cognito resources, starting the containerized stack, and bootstrapping local DynamoDB.

decisions.title: ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript, and Vitest
decisions.summary: ESLint is used for linting with Vue and TypeScript configurations. Prettier is employed for code formatting. TypeScript is enabled for strict type checking. Vitest is chosen for unit testing with coverage reporting.

rules.rule: Core Game Loop: Pull items, craft, store or sell, and repeat with improved items.
Leverage AWS Bedrock models (Nova Pro, Claude Sonnet 3.7/4) for item generation, crafting logic, appraisal, and image generation.
Core Game Loop: Pull items, craft, store or sell, and repeat with improved items.
Leverage AWS Bedrock models (Nova Pro, Claude Sonnet 3.7/4) for item generation, crafting logic, appraisal, and image generation.
Manages WebSocket communication
Handles collision detection and movement
Tracks on-screen objects
Manage game items and storage
WebSocket messages follow kebab-case naming pattern
Server handlers are organized by message type
Event-driven frontend updates maintain reactive state
Each service has its own `iac/` directory for Infrastructure as Code
Server tests are placed in `__tests__/` directories alongside source code
Client tests use Vitest with Vue Test Utils
File names, component names, and message types follow kebab-case
JavaScript/TypeScript variables and functions use camelCase
Vue components and TypeScript interfaces use PascalCase
